name: Pork Pie
recipeInstructions:
1. Melt lard in a pan. Add 300ml water and bring to the boil.
2. Using a fork, stir the liquid into the flour and salt to make a dough.
3. Knead the dough until smooth.
4. Reserve a quarter of the dough and keep it warm (covered with a bowl).
5. Mix together chopped pork, onion, mixed herbs, and salt.
6. Line the base of a 1kg loaf tin with the remaining dough.
7. Turn the pork into the tin. Roll out the reserved pastry and use for the lid.
8. Seal the pastry edges firmly, then trim off surplus (use to make decoration).
9. Crimp the edges.
10. Glaze the pie with beaten egg and make two air holes.
11. Bake at 220°C/425°F or Gas Mark 7 for 30 minutes.
12. Reduce the heat to 190°C/375°F or Gas Mark 5 and bake for 1 hour.
13. Cool in the tin for 30 minutes, then turn onto a wire rack.
14. Dissolve gelatin in stock and carefully pour into the pie using a funnel.
15. Leave to set. Serve with salad.
